TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == Al Bianchi == siwkay niza Al Bianchi (3 a bulad 26 a demiad, 1932 a mihcan), tatama, miunduay ku kawaw niza. mimali i Amilka NBA. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Al Bianchi is a former professional basketball player, coach, general manager, consultant and scout. Nicknamed "Blinky", he attended P.S. 4 elementary school, and graduated from Long Island City High School in 1950. A 1954 graduate of Bowling Green State University, he was voted to the "All-Ohio Team" and received honorable mention as a basketball All-American. He served in the U.S. Army Medical Corp from 1954 to 1956. Starting in 1956, he played for the Syracuse Nationals of the NBA. He moved with the team to Philadelphia when it became the 76ers for the 1963‚Äď64 season. He was one of the last proponents in the NBA of the two-handed set shot. kakuniza kalunasulitan-sulu
S.PHON: == ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi == siwkaj niza ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi (* a bur~…ĺad ** a d…ômiad, **** a mińß ¶an), tatama, miunduaj ku kawaw niza. mimar~…ĺi i amir~…ĺka nba. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi is a form…ôr prof…ôssionar~…ĺ bask…ôtbar~…ĺr~…ĺ pr~…ĺaj…ôr, ¶oa ¶ńß, g…ôn…ôrar~…ĺ manag…ôr, ¶onsur~…ĺtant and s ¶out. ni ¶knam…ôd "br~…ĺinkj", ńß…ô att…ônd…ôd p.s. * …ôr~…ĺ…ôm…ôntarj s ¶ńßoor~…ĺ, and graduat…ôd from r~…ĺoŇč isr~…ĺand ¶itj ńßigńß s ¶ńßoor~…ĺ in ****. a **** graduat…ô of bowr~…ĺiŇč gr…ô…ôn stat…ô uni*…ôrsitj, ńß…ô was *ot…ôd to tńß…ô "ar~…ĺr~…ĺ-ońßio t…ôam" and r…ô ¶…ôi*…ôd ńßonorabr~…ĺ…ô m…ôntion as a bask…ôtbar~…ĺr~…ĺ ar~…ĺr~…ĺ-am…ôri ¶an. ńß…ô s…ôr*…ôd in tńß…ô u.s. armj m…ôdi ¶ar~…ĺ ¶orp from **** to ****. startiŇč in ****, ńß…ô pr~…ĺaj…ôd for tńß…ô sjra ¶us…ô nationar~…ĺs of tńß…ô nba. ńß…ô mo*…ôd witńß tńß…ô t…ôam to pńßir~…ĺad…ôr~…ĺpńßia wńß…ôn it b…ô ¶am…ô tńß…ô **…ôrs for tńß…ô ******* s…ôason. ńß…ô was on…ô of tńß…ô r~…ĺast propon…ônts in tńß…ô nba of tńß…ô two-ńßand…ôd s…ôt sńßot. kakuniza kar~…ĺunasur~…ĺitan-sur~…ĺu
S.FORM: == Al Bianchi == siwkay niza Al Bianchi (3 a bulad 26 a demiad, 1932 a mihcan), tatama, miunduay ku kawaw niza. mimali i Amilka NBA. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Al Bianchi is a former professional basketball player, coach, general manager, consultant and scout. Nicknamed "Blinky", he attended P.S. 4 elementary school, and graduated from Long Island City High School in 1950. A 1954 graduate of Bowling Green State University, he was voted to the "All-Ohio Team" and received honorable mention as a basketball All-American. He served in the U.S. Army Medical Corp from 1954 to 1956. Starting in 1956, he played for the Syracuse Nationals of the NBA. He moved with the team to Philadelphia when it became the 76ers for the 1963‚Äď64 season. He was one of the last proponents in the NBA of the two-handed set shot. kakuniza kalunasulitan-sulu
S.PHON: == ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi == siwkaj niza ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi (* a bur~…ĺad ** a d…ômiad, **** a mińß ¶an), tatama, miunduaj ku kawaw niza. mimar~…ĺi i amir~…ĺka nba. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika ar~…ĺ bian ¶ńßi is a form…ôr prof…ôssionar~…ĺ bask…ôtbar~…ĺr~…ĺ pr~…ĺaj…ôr, ¶oa ¶ńß, g…ôn…ôrar~…ĺ manag…ôr, ¶onsur~…ĺtant and s ¶out. ni ¶knam…ôd "br~…ĺinkj", ńß…ô att…ônd…ôd p.s. * …ôr~…ĺ…ôm…ôntarj s ¶ńßoor~…ĺ, and graduat…ôd from r~…ĺoŇč isr~…ĺand ¶itj ńßigńß s ¶ńßoor~…ĺ in ****. a **** graduat…ô of bowr~…ĺiŇč gr…ô…ôn stat…ô uni*…ôrsitj, ńß…ô was *ot…ôd to tńß…ô "ar~…ĺr~…ĺ-ońßio t…ôam" and r…ô ¶…ôi*…ôd ńßonorabr~…ĺ…ô m…ôntion as a bask…ôtbar~…ĺr~…ĺ ar~…ĺr~…ĺ-am…ôri ¶an. ńß…ô s…ôr*…ôd in tńß…ô u.s. armj m…ôdi ¶ar~…ĺ ¶orp from **** to ****. startiŇč in ****, ńß…ô pr~…ĺaj…ôd for tńß…ô sjra ¶us…ô nationar~…ĺs of tńß…ô nba. ńß…ô mo*…ôd witńß tńß…ô t…ôam to pńßir~…ĺad…ôr~…ĺpńßia wńß…ôn it b…ô ¶am…ô tńß…ô **…ôrs for tńß…ô ******* s…ôason. ńß…ô was on…ô of tńß…ô r~…ĺast propon…ônts in tńß…ô nba of tńß…ô two-ńßand…ôd s…ôt sńßot. kakuniza kar~…ĺunasur~…ĺitan-sur~…ĺu
